_archived: Archived 2026-06-28 — point-in-time pre-build DDL design spec. The analytics projection SHIPPED in supabase/migrations/20260627000000_baseline_schema.sql (helpers inlined into reconcile_user; cron times and seed count differ from this spec). Historical — the baseline migration is the source of truth. Deferred scaling items were moved to supabase/migrations/README.md.
overview: Add a normalized, append-derived, set-level analytics PROJECTION (public.analytics_sessions + public.analytics_session_sets, plus public.analytics_profile and public.exercise_facts) ALONGSIDE the existing public.daily_rep_state blob. The blob stays the offline-first source of truth (client LWW + existing BEFORE-UPDATE monotonic guard); the projection is a deterministic, eventually-consistent function of the blob, derived ENTIRELY server-side (client dual-write rejected). Write-path: a trivial BEFORE/AFTER trigger on daily_rep_state enqueues the user; a pg_cron drain (1 min) and sweep (15 min, plus nightly full sweep) call SECURITY DEFINER analytics.reconcile_user(uid), which sanitizes the blob (mirroring the client's sanitizeWorkout/repairExercise), diffs each completed Workout by a canonical projection-relevant fingerprint, and re-explodes only changed sessions (DELETE+reinsert that session's sets) while hard-deleting sessions absent from the blob. CRITICAL CORRECTION over the draft: the change-watermark is the SERVER-set daily_rep_state.updated_at (now()), NOT the client-controlled client_updated_at, because the existing guard can accept stale content carrying an inflated client clock and would otherwise wedge the projection permanently. Derived math (effectiveLoad with bw*0.65 inner round, Epley e1RM, unrounded kg) is computed once in plpgsql over NUMERIC and unit-tested for bit-equality against stats.ts/format.ts; both display-unit and unrounded-kg columns are stored so per-user views and cohort matviews are pure SUM/MAX. Per-user reads go through public security_invoker views that ALSO carry their own auth.uid() WHERE filter (belt-and-suspenders against the owner-bypass footgun); cohort metrics are private matviews/rollups in an analytics schema with no client USAGE, surfaced only via no-arg SECURITY DEFINER RPCs with a K-anonymity floor. Every SECURITY DEFINER function uses search_path=pg_catalog,pg_temp with fully schema-qualified names (authenticated has CREATE on public + USAGE on extensions, so an unqualified name is an escalation vector). All projection tables get RLS+FORCE, revoked client writes (defense-in-depth, not relying on a single REVOKE), and SELECT-only client grants.

tables.name: public.exercise_facts
tables.purpose: Server-side mirror of the two catalogue-derived facts stats.ts needs (is_bodyweight_lift classification and muscle->region set). Seeded mechanically from src/data/exercises.ts + src/data/muscles.ts at migration time (NEVER hand-transcribed). Read-only reference data; client write FORBIDDEN (it feeds every user's derived columns, so a poisoned row would corrupt cohort metrics).

tables.name: public.analytics_sessions
tables.purpose: Grain = one COMPLETED Workout. Pre-aggregated working totals (done AND NOT warmup) so headline volume/adherence never scan sets. Natural composite PK (user_id, session_id) for trivial idempotent upsert + FK cascade. RLS-scoped to owner on read; client SELECT-only; rows enter only via the SECURITY DEFINER extractor from the user's own blob, so write-forgery is structurally impossible.
tables.ddl:
CREATE TABLE public.analytics_sessions (
  user_id           uuid        NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  session_id        text        NOT NULL,                 -- = Workout.id
  performed_at      timestamptz NOT NULL,                 -- to_timestamp(COALESCE(completedAt,date)/1000.0)
  performed_on      date        NOT NULL,                 -- LOCAL calendar day (client tzOffsetMin; UTC fallback, see writePath)
  local_week        integer     NOT NULL,                 -- floor((epoch_local_day+3)/7), matches localWeek()
  day_is_estimated  boolean     NOT NULL DEFAULT false,   -- true when performed_on came from the UTC fallback (no client tzOffset)
  started_at        timestamptz,
  completed_at      timestamptz,
  title             text        NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  unit              text        NOT NULL CHECK (unit IN ('lb','kg')),  -- profile.unit at projection time
  bodyweight        numeric(7,2),                          -- RESOLVED bodyweight used for math (frozen Workout.bodyweight, else profile bw, else default 155lb/70kg)
  bodyweight_kg     numeric(7,2),                          -- unrounded bodyweight/2.20462 when unit=lb else bodyweight
  duration_min      integer,
  focus             text[]      NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  gen_focus         text[],
  plan_id           text,
  plan_day_label    text,
  working_volume    numeric(14,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,      -- sum set_volume (display unit)
  working_volume_kg numeric(14,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  working_set_count integer       NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  working_rep_count integer       NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  exercise_count    integer       NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,      -- exercises with >=1 working set
  fingerprint       text        NOT NULL,                 -- sha256 of CANONICAL projection-relevant subtree (see triggersFunctions)
  blob_updated_at   timestamptz NOT NULL,                 -- SERVER daily_rep_state.updated_at that produced this row
  schema_version    integer     NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,       -- formula/catalogue version; part of the re-explode skip predicate
  projected_at      timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  PRIMARY KEY (user_id, session_id)
);
ALTER TABLE public.analytics_sessions ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.analytics_sessions FORCE  ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
REVOKE ALL                    ON public.analytics_sessions FROM anon, authenticated;
GRANT  SELECT                 ON public.analytics_sessions TO authenticated;
CREATE POLICY sess_read ON public.analytics_sessions FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING ((select auth.uid()) = user_id);
-- deliberately ZERO insert/update/delete policy + FORCE RLS => clients cannot write even if a grant leaks.

tables.name: public.analytics_session_sets
tables.purpose: Grain = one LoggedSet. Flattened WorkoutExercise context onto each set (no session_exercises table). Stores RAW logged inputs (display unit) plus extractor-computed DERIVED columns (display + unrounded kg) so all metric views/matviews are pure SUM/MAX with no SQL re-implementation of effectiveLoad/Epley/unit math. FK cascade to sessions. Only table that scales (~20-40 sets/session); ship the 4 indexes below, partition only at the data-driven threshold.
tables.ddl:
CREATE TABLE public.analytics_session_sets (
  user_id            uuid        NOT NULL,
  session_id         text        NOT NULL,
  set_id             text        NOT NULL,                -- = LoggedSet.id
  performed_at       timestamptz NOT NULL,                -- denormalized from session
  performed_on       date        NOT NULL,
  exercise_id        text        NOT NULL,                -- plain text, NO FK (client-bundled catalogue)
  instance_id        text,
  plan_lift_id       text,
  plan_slot          integer,
  superset_group     text,
  exercise_order     integer     NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  set_order          integer     NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  target_rep_low     integer,
  target_rep_high    integer,
  regions            text[]      NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',   -- from exercise_facts; '{}' for unknown ids (contributes 0 to region views, matching stats.ts !ex continue)
  is_bodyweight_lift boolean     NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  unit               text        NOT NULL CHECK (unit IN ('lb','kg')),
  weight             numeric(9,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,     -- raw external load, display unit
  reps               integer      NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  rpe                numeric(3,1),
  done               boolean      NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  warmup             boolean      NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  is_working         boolean      GENERATED ALWAYS AS (done AND NOT warmup) STORED,
  effective_weight    numeric(11,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- round(bw*0.65)+weight when bodyweight-lift & bw>0 else weight (display unit)
  effective_weight_kg numeric(11,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- unrounded /2.20462 from effective_weight's components
  e1rm               numeric(11,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,    -- Epley over EFFECTIVE load (display unit)
  e1rm_kg            numeric(11,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  set_volume         numeric(14,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,    -- effective_weight*reps when is_working else 0 (display unit)
  set_volume_kg      numeric(14,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  schema_version     integer       NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  PRIMARY KEY (user_id, session_id, set_id),
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id, session_id)
     REFERENCES public.analytics_sessions(user_id, session_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);
ALTER TABLE public.analytics_session_sets ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.analytics_session_sets FORCE  ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
REVOKE ALL    ON public.analytics_session_sets FROM anon, authenticated;
GRANT  SELECT ON public.analytics_session_sets TO authenticated;
CREATE POLICY sets_read ON public.analytics_session_sets FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING ((select auth.uid()) = user_id);

tables.name: public.analytics_profile
tables.purpose: One row per user projecting the few profile fields the per-user metric views need (daysPerWeek for adherence, unit, bodyweight). Populated by the same reconcile_user pass. Keeps adherence/region views self-contained without re-reading the blob.

tables.name: analytics (private schema) + bookkeeping tables
tables.purpose: Private schema holding cohort matviews/rollups, the reconcile queue, per-user watermark, and a meta(catalogue/schema version) row. NO USAGE for anon/authenticated, so matviews (which cannot carry RLS) are protected purely by schema-USAGE denial. Clients never reach in; SECURITY DEFINER functions and service_role do.

indexes: CREATE INDEX sessions_user_time_idx  ON public.analytics_sessions (user_id, performed_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX sessions_user_week_idx  ON public.analytics_sessions (user_id, local_week);
CREATE INDEX sessions_plan_idx       ON public.analytics_sessions (plan_id) WHERE plan_id IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX sets_user_ex_time_idx   ON public.analytics_session_sets (user_id, exercise_id, performed_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX sets_working_idx        ON public.analytics_session_sets (user_id, performed_at DESC) WHERE is_working;
CREATE INDEX sets_exercise_cohort_idx ON public.analytics_session_sets (exercise_id) WHERE is_working;

-- NOTE: the draft's sets_regions_gin is DROPPED. region views use unnest(regions)+GROUP BY which a GIN index never serves; regions is a 4-value domain. The sets_working_idx partial index already serves the WHERE is_working scan. Add a regions GIN ONLY if a containment drill-down (regions @> ARRAY['legs']) becomes a hot query.
rlsPolicies: ALTER TABLE public.analytics_sessions     ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.analytics_sessions     FORCE  ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.analytics_session_sets ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.analytics_session_sets FORCE  ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.analytics_profile      ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.analytics_profile      FORCE  ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.exercise_facts         ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.exercise_facts         FORCE  ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
-- CRITICAL default-ACL neutralization (public default ACL grants authenticated=arwd at CREATE; confirmed live). Run in the SAME migration/transaction as each CREATE TABLE:
REVOKE ALL ON public.analytics_sessions, public.analytics_session_sets, public.analytics_profile FROM anon, authenticated;
GRANT  SELECT ON public.analytics_sessions, public.analytics_session_sets, public.analytics_profile TO authenticated;
CREATE POLICY sess_read    ON public.analytics_sessions     FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING ((select auth.uid()) = user_id);
CREATE POLICY sets_read    ON public.analytics_session_sets FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING ((select auth.uid()) = user_id);
CREATE POLICY profile_read ON public.analytics_profile      FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING ((select auth.uid()) = user_id);
-- NO insert/update/delete policies exist on any projection table; with FORCE RLS this denies ALL client writes even if a grant leaks (defense-in-depth, not relying on the single REVOKE).
-- Function EXECUTE lockdown (functions default-grant EXECUTE to PUBLIC; must revoke):
REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION analytics.reconcile_user(uuid)           FROM PUBLIC, anon, authenticated;
REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION analytics.drain_reconcile_queue(integer) FROM PUBLIC, anon, authenticated;
REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION analytics.sweep_stale(integer, boolean)  FROM PUBLIC, anon, authenticated;
REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION analytics.backfill_projection(uuid)      FROM PUBLIC, anon, authenticated;
REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION analytics.tg_enqueue_reconcile()         FROM PUBLIC, anon, authenticated;
GRANT  EXECUTE ON FUNCTION analytics.reconcile_user(uuid), analytics.drain_reconcile_queue(integer), analytics.sweep_stale(integer, boolean), analytics.backfill_projection(uuid) TO service_role;
-- Cohort RPCs: no-arg, definer, K-anon; revoke anon, grant authenticated only:
REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION public.my_volume_percentile()  FROM PUBLIC, anon;
GRANT  EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.my_volume_percentile() TO authenticated;
-- Per-user views MUST be security_invoker AND carry their own auth.uid() filter (belt-and-suspenders vs owner-bypass).
triggersFunctions:
-- =====================================================================
-- CHANGE-DETECTOR TRIGGER on daily_rep_state. Fires enqueue ONLY on a real WINNING change.
-- The existing BEFORE guard reverts NEW.data/NEW.client_updated_at on a stale push, so
-- WHEN (NEW.data IS DISTINCT FROM OLD.data) is FALSE exactly for guard-rejected / no-op writes.
-- Scope to the workouts subtree so unrelated profile/plan edits never enqueue a full re-diff.
-- =====================================================================
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION analytics.tg_enqueue_reconcile()
RETURNS trigger LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = pg_catalog, pg_temp AS $$
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO analytics.reconcile_queue(user_id) VALUES (NEW.user_id)
  ON CONFLICT (user_id) DO UPDATE SET enqueued_at = now();
  RETURN NULL;
END $$;
CREATE TRIGGER daily_rep_state_enqueue_analytics_ins
  AFTER INSERT ON public.daily_rep_state
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION analytics.tg_enqueue_reconcile();
CREATE TRIGGER daily_rep_state_enqueue_analytics_upd
  AFTER UPDATE ON public.daily_rep_state
  FOR EACH ROW
  WHEN (NEW.data -> 'workouts' IS DISTINCT FROM OLD.data -> 'workouts')
  EXECUTE FUNCTION analytics.tg_enqueue_reconcile();

-- =====================================================================
-- is_projectable: mirrors client sanitizeWorkout/repairExercise so the projection ingests
-- EXACTLY the sessions/exercises the client retains. exercises must be an array; an exercise
-- counts only if its sets is an array (targetReps is repaired, not load-bearing); session is
-- projectable iff >=1 exercise survives. Used IDENTICALLY by the delete check and the upsert.
-- =====================================================================
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION analytics.is_projectable(w jsonb)
RETURNS boolean LANGUAGE sql IMMUTABLE SET search_path = pg_catalog, pg_temp AS $$
  SELECT w->>'status' = 'completed'
     AND jsonb_typeof(w->'exercises') = 'array'
     AND EXISTS (
       SELECT 1 FROM jsonb_array_elements(w->'exercises') e
       WHERE jsonb_typeof(e->'sets') = 'array');
$$;

-- =====================================================================
-- canonical_fingerprint: hash ONLY projection-relevant fields, normalized, so the re-explode
-- fires iff a projected value actually changed. Excludes note/coaching/group ordering jitter and
-- numeric representation jitter (cast through numeric). Includes unit so a setUnit weight rewrite
-- still flips it. Exercises sorted (plan_slot, exercise_id, instance_id); sets by set_order/set_id.
-- =====================================================================
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION analytics.canonical_fingerprint(w jsonb, p_unit text)
RETURNS text LANGUAGE sql IMMUTABLE SET search_path = pg_catalog, extensions, pg_temp AS $$
  SELECT encode(extensions.digest(convert_to(
    jsonb_build_object(
      'id', w->>'id', 'status', w->>'status',
      'completedAt', w->>'completedAt', 'date', w->>'date',
      'bodyweight', (w->>'bodyweight'),
      'performed_on', w->>'performed_on', 'tz', w->>'tzOffsetMin', 'unit', p_unit,
      'ex', (
        SELECT jsonb_agg(jsonb_build_object(
                 'xid', e->>'exerciseId',
                 'sets', (SELECT jsonb_agg(jsonb_build_object(
                            'sid', s->>'id',
                            'w', (s->>'weight')::numeric,
                            'r', (s->>'reps')::numeric,
                            'd', COALESCE((s->>'done')::boolean,false),
                            'wu', COALESCE((s->>'warmup')::boolean,false),
                            'rpe', (s->>'rpe'))
                          ORDER BY COALESCE((s->>'id'),''))
                          FROM jsonb_array_elements(e->'sets') s))
               ORDER BY COALESCE((e->>'planSlot'),''), COALESCE((e->>'exerciseId'),''), COALESCE((e->>'instanceId'),''))
        FROM jsonb_array_elements(w->'exercises') e
        WHERE jsonb_typeof(e->'sets') = 'array')
    )::text, 'UTF8'), 'sha256'), 'hex');
$$;

-- =====================================================================
-- _project_changed: explode one user's CHANGED completed sessions. Skips a session iff its
-- fingerprint matches AND its stored schema_version = current (so a formula/catalogue bump
-- forces re-derive). All math NUMERIC; mirrors stats.ts/format.ts bit-for-bit.
--   eff_disp := CASE WHEN isbw THEN round(bw*0.65) + weight ELSE weight END        -- inner round
--   to_kg(v) := CASE WHEN unit='kg' THEN v ELSE v / 2.20462 END                    -- UNROUNDED
--   e1rm_disp:= CASE WHEN eff_disp = 0 OR reps = 0 THEN 0                           -- guard on EFFECTIVE load
--                    WHEN reps = 1 THEN eff_disp                                    -- NO round at reps=1
--                    ELSE round(eff_disp * (1 + reps::numeric/30)) END              -- Epley, OUTER round, 30 as numeric
--   set_vol  := CASE WHEN done AND NOT warmup THEN eff_disp*reps ELSE 0 END
-- isbw := COALESCE(ef.is_bodyweight_lift,false) AND bw_resolved > 0  (unknown id -> regions '{}', no bw bump, set still volumes at raw weight, matching workoutVolume)
-- bw_resolved := COALESCE(workout.bodyweight, profile.bodyweight, defaultBodyweight(unit)=155 lb / 70 kg)  -- matches stats.ts caller fallback chain
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION analytics._project_changed(p_user_id uuid, p_data jsonb, p_unit text, p_blob_updated_at timestamptz)
RETURNS integer LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = pg_catalog, extensions, pg_temp AS $$
DECLARE w jsonb; v_fp text; v_sv int; v_changed int := 0;
        v_prof_bw numeric; v_bw numeric; v_perf_at timestamptz; v_perf_on date; v_week int; v_estd boolean;
BEGIN
  SELECT meta.schema_version INTO v_sv FROM analytics.meta meta WHERE meta.id = 1;
  v_prof_bw := NULLIF(p_data->'profile'->>'bodyweight','')::numeric;
  FOR w IN SELECT e FROM jsonb_array_elements(p_data->'workouts') e WHERE analytics.is_projectable(e) LOOP
    v_fp := analytics.canonical_fingerprint(w, p_unit);
    -- skip iff unchanged AND current schema_version
    PERFORM 1 FROM public.analytics_sessions s
      WHERE s.user_id=p_user_id AND s.session_id = w->>'id' AND s.fingerprint=v_fp AND s.schema_version=v_sv;
    IF FOUND THEN CONTINUE; END IF;
    -- resolved bodyweight (frozen -> profile -> default)
    v_bw := COALESCE(NULLIF(w->>'bodyweight','')::numeric, v_prof_bw, CASE WHEN p_unit='kg' THEN 70 ELSE 155 END);
    -- performed_at / performed_on / local_week (see writePath for tz provenance)
    v_perf_at := to_timestamp(COALESCE((w->>'completedAt'),(w->>'date'))::numeric / 1000.0);
    IF (w->>'performed_on') IS NOT NULL THEN
      v_perf_on := (w->>'performed_on')::date; v_estd := false;
    ELSIF (w->>'tzOffsetMin') IS NOT NULL THEN
      v_perf_on := to_timestamp((COALESCE((w->>'completedAt'),(w->>'date'))::numeric - (w->>'tzOffsetMin')::numeric*60000)/1000.0)::date; v_estd := false;
    ELSE
      v_perf_on := (v_perf_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC')::date; v_estd := true;
    END IF;
    v_week := floor(((v_perf_on - DATE '1970-01-01') + 3)::numeric / 7);
    -- re-explode this session: delete old sets, upsert header, insert sets with NUMERIC math
    DELETE FROM public.analytics_session_sets x WHERE x.user_id=p_user_id AND x.session_id = w->>'id';
    INSERT INTO public.analytics_sessions AS s (user_id, session_id, performed_at, performed_on, local_week, day_is_estimated,
        started_at, completed_at, title, unit, bodyweight, bodyweight_kg, duration_min, focus, gen_focus, plan_id, plan_day_label,
        working_volume, working_volume_kg, working_set_count, working_rep_count, exercise_count, fingerprint, blob_updated_at, schema_version, projected_at)
      SELECT p_user_id, w->>'id', v_perf_at, v_perf_on, v_week, v_estd,
        to_timestamp(NULLIF(w->>'startedAt','')::numeric/1000.0), to_timestamp(NULLIF(w->>'completedAt','')::numeric/1000.0),
        COALESCE(w->>'title',''), p_unit, v_bw, CASE WHEN p_unit='kg' THEN v_bw ELSE v_bw/2.20462 END,
        NULLIF(w->>'durationMin','')::int,
        COALESCE(ARRAY(SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(w->'focus')),'{}'), ARRAY(SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(w->'genFocus')),
        w->>'planId', w->>'planDayLabel',
        agg.vol, agg.vol_kg, agg.sets, agg.reps, agg.exs, v_fp, p_blob_updated_at, v_sv, now()
      FROM (
        SELECT
          COALESCE(sum(CASE WHEN d.done AND NOT d.warmup THEN d.eff*d.reps END),0) AS vol,
          COALESCE(sum(CASE WHEN d.done AND NOT d.warmup THEN d.eff_kg*d.reps END),0) AS vol_kg,
          COALESCE(sum(CASE WHEN d.done AND NOT d.warmup THEN 1 END),0) AS sets,
          COALESCE(sum(CASE WHEN d.done AND NOT d.warmup THEN d.reps END),0) AS reps,
          count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN d.done AND NOT d.warmup THEN d.exord END) AS exs
        FROM analytics._explode_sets(p_user_id, w, p_unit, v_bw, v_perf_at, v_perf_on, v_sv) d
      ) agg
      ON CONFLICT (user_id, session_id) DO UPDATE SET
        performed_at=EXCLUDED.performed_at, performed_on=EXCLUDED.performed_on, local_week=EXCLUDED.local_week,
        day_is_estimated=EXCLUDED.day_is_estimated, started_at=EXCLUDED.started_at, completed_at=EXCLUDED.completed_at,
        title=EXCLUDED.title, unit=EXCLUDED.unit, bodyweight=EXCLUDED.bodyweight, bodyweight_kg=EXCLUDED.bodyweight_kg,
        duration_min=EXCLUDED.duration_min, focus=EXCLUDED.focus, gen_focus=EXCLUDED.gen_focus, plan_id=EXCLUDED.plan_id,
        plan_day_label=EXCLUDED.plan_day_label, working_volume=EXCLUDED.working_volume, working_volume_kg=EXCLUDED.working_volume_kg,
        working_set_count=EXCLUDED.working_set_count, working_rep_count=EXCLUDED.working_rep_count, exercise_count=EXCLUDED.exercise_count,
        fingerprint=EXCLUDED.fingerprint, blob_updated_at=EXCLUDED.blob_updated_at, schema_version=EXCLUDED.schema_version, projected_at=now();
    -- insert the set rows (same _explode_sets expansion)
    INSERT INTO public.analytics_session_sets (user_id, session_id, set_id, performed_at, performed_on, exercise_id, instance_id,
        plan_lift_id, plan_slot, superset_group, exercise_order, set_order, target_rep_low, target_rep_high, regions, is_bodyweight_lift,
        unit, weight, reps, rpe, done, warmup, effective_weight, effective_weight_kg, e1rm, e1rm_kg, set_volume, set_volume_kg, schema_version)
      SELECT p_user_id, w->>'id', d.set_id, v_perf_at, v_perf_on, d.exercise_id, d.instance_id, d.plan_lift_id, d.plan_slot,
        d.superset_group, d.exord, d.sord, d.tr_low, d.tr_high, d.regions, d.isbw, p_unit, d.weight, d.reps, d.rpe, d.done, d.warmup,
        d.eff, d.eff_kg,
        CASE WHEN d.eff=0 OR d.reps=0 THEN 0 WHEN d.reps=1 THEN d.eff ELSE round(d.eff*(1+d.reps::numeric/30)) END,
        CASE WHEN d.eff_kg=0 OR d.reps=0 THEN 0 WHEN d.reps=1 THEN d.eff_kg ELSE round(d.eff_kg*(1+d.reps::numeric/30)) END,
        CASE WHEN d.done AND NOT d.warmup THEN d.eff*d.reps ELSE 0 END,
        CASE WHEN d.done AND NOT d.warmup THEN d.eff_kg*d.reps ELSE 0 END, v_sv
      FROM analytics._explode_sets(p_user_id, w, p_unit, v_bw, v_perf_at, v_perf_on, v_sv) d;
    v_changed := v_changed + 1;
  END LOOP;
  RETURN v_changed;
END $$;

-- drain: dequeue+reconcile. On error, RE-ENQUEUE (do not strand until sweep). Per-user reconcile
-- is its own subtransaction via the BEGIN/EXCEPTION block.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION analytics.drain_reconcile_queue(p_limit int DEFAULT 200)
RETURNS int LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = pg_catalog, extensions, pg_temp AS $$
DECLARE r record; n int := 0; BEGIN
  FOR r IN DELETE FROM analytics.reconcile_queue
           WHERE user_id IN (SELECT user_id FROM analytics.reconcile_queue ORDER BY enqueued_at LIMIT p_limit)
           RETURNING user_id LOOP
    BEGIN
      PERFORM analytics.reconcile_user(r.user_id); n := n + 1;
    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
      INSERT INTO analytics.reconcile_queue(user_id) VALUES (r.user_id) ON CONFLICT (user_id) DO UPDATE SET enqueued_at = now();
      INSERT INTO analytics.user_watermark(user_id,last_status,last_error,retry_count)
      VALUES (r.user_id,'error',SQLERRM,1)
      ON CONFLICT (user_id) DO UPDATE SET last_status='error', last_error=SQLERRM, retry_count=analytics.user_watermark.retry_count+1;
    END;
  END LOOP; RETURN n; END $$;

-- sweep: catch-all driven by SERVER updated_at vs monotonic watermark. p_full=true ignores watermark
-- (nightly self-heal for missed enqueues / falsely-advanced watermarks / out-of-band writes).
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION analytics.sweep_stale(p_limit int DEFAULT 500, p_full boolean DEFAULT false)
RETURNS int LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = pg_catalog, extensions, pg_temp AS $$
DECLARE r record; n int := 0; BEGIN
  FOR r IN SELECT d.user_id FROM public.daily_rep_state d
           LEFT JOIN analytics.user_watermark w USING (user_id)
           WHERE jsonb_typeof(d.data->'workouts') = 'array'
             AND (p_full OR w.last_blob_updated_at IS NULL OR d.updated_at > w.last_blob_updated_at)
           ORDER BY d.updated_at LIMIT (CASE WHEN p_full THEN 1000000 ELSE p_limit END) LOOP
    PERFORM analytics.reconcile_user(r.user_id); n := n + 1;
  END LOOP; RETURN n; END $$;

writePath.approach: SERVER-SIDE EXTRACTOR, not client dual-write. The client's existing sync.ts pushNow() upsert of public.daily_rep_state is UNCHANGED and remains the only client->server write. Derivation is a deterministic function of the blob, run by analytics.reconcile_user() under pg_cron. Three blob mutations have no per-entity client event (deleteWorkout = hard array filter with no tombstone; setUnit rewrites every historical weight in place; mergePersisted/pullAndReconcile swaps the whole workouts[] on sign-in/visibility), so a client dual-write would have to reimplement the server diff plus a second outbox/retry/auth scheme — strictly more code and more divergence surface. The projection is explicitly eventually-consistent (lags blob by <=~1 min); per-user 'now' screens keep reading the blob via stats.ts.
writePath.clientChanges: EXACTLY ONE additive, optional change, in src/store/useStore.ts finishWorkout (~line 527, where the completed Workout is built and pushed into workouts[]): stamp two new optional fields on the completed Workout object — performed_on (YYYY-MM-DD local calendar day, computed as new Date(completedAt) with the device tz, formatted to local date) AND tzOffsetMin (new Date(completedAt).getTimezoneOffset() at completion). Both are additive optional fields on the Workout type in src/types.ts; no new write path, no migration — they ride the existing blob push. tzOffsetMin lets the server recompute local day deterministically (performed_on := ((completedAt - tzOffsetMin*60000)) local date) and survives a later device-tz change; performed_on is the human-readable cross-check. No other client file changes. sync.ts, pushNow, mergePersisted, deleteWorkout, setUnit are untouched. Per the §6 ownership decision: per-user weekly-streak and adherence views are NOT authoritative — the client's stats.ts (which uses the live getTimezoneOffset) remains the source of truth shown on-device; the projection's streak/adherence are for cross-session/admin context and are tz-robust for volume/e1RM/region (date-bucketed) and only edge-cosmetic for streak near midnight on pre-tzOffset blobs.
writePath.idempotency: Per-session canonical fingerprint = sha256 over ONLY the projection-relevant subtree (id, status, completedAt/date, bodyweight, performed_on/tzOffsetMin, unit, and per exercise its exerciseId + ordered sets of {set_id, weight::numeric, reps::numeric, done, warmup, rpe}), with exercises sorted (plan_slot, exercise_id, instance_id) and sets sorted by id, and numbers cast through numeric to kill 1.0-vs-1 jitter. A session is re-exploded (DELETE its sets + reinsert) iff its fingerprint changed OR its stored schema_version != current analytics.meta.schema_version. Thus: a pure note/coaching/array-reorder edit => zero churn; a setUnit weight rewrite => fingerprint flips => re-explode with kg; a formula or exercise_facts reseed => bump analytics.meta.schema_version => next reconcile/backfill re-derives every row. Delete signal = session_id absent from blob as a PROJECTABLE completed workout (is_projectable used identically by the delete check and the upsert, so keep/delete never desync). The watermark is the SERVER updated_at (now(), set by the existing BEFORE trigger, monotonic and skew-proof); it is written monotonically and the sweep re-reconciles whenever blob.updated_at > watermark. The queue is a latency optimization only — the updated_at-vs-watermark sweep is the correctness floor (max staleness = sweep interval; nightly full sweep is the absolute self-heal). A lost queue entry, a failed drain, or a multi-device race is therefore harmless.
writePath.offlineBehavior: All offline tolerance is inherited from the blob: edits/deletes made offline land in the projection only when the device next syncs and the winning blob is reconciled — the same guarantee the blob itself has. Cohort metrics are documented as eventually-consistent: a session deleted on a device that stays offline keeps contributing to cohort aggregates until that device reconnects, bounded by the nightly full sweep for any missed/lost signal. The reconciler never holds a row lock on daily_rep_state (plain READ COMMITTED snapshot read), so a slow re-explode never stalls the user's live sync push. CRITICAL: the projection must NEVER read data->'current' (the active in-progress session, which IS synced via partialize and can diverge across devices under LWW) — only data->'workouts' with is_projectable, so an active session can never leak into per-user or cohort stats.

backfill: "analytics.backfill_projection(p_user_id uuid DEFAULT NULL) := 'run the projector for everyone' — there is NO separate backfill logic, so there is no backfill-vs-live drift. It loops users and PERFORMs analytics.reconcile_user(uid). Re-runnable: the fingerprint+schema_version skip makes a second run a near-no-op (zero row churn). A formula or exercise_facts change => bump analytics.meta.schema_version, then run backfill to re-derive all rows. DDL: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION analytics.backfill_projection(p_user_id uuid DEFAULT NULL) RETURNS bigint LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = pg_catalog, extensions, pg_temp AS $$ DECLARE r record; n bigint := 0; BEGIN FOR r IN SELECT user_id FROM public.daily_rep_state WHERE p_user_id IS NULL OR user_id = p_user_id ORDER BY user_id LOOP PERFORM analytics.reconcile_user(r.user_id); n := n+1; END LOOP; RETURN n; END $$; REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION analytics.backfill_projection(uuid) FROM PUBLIC, anon, authenticated; GRANT EXECUTE TO service_role. CRITICAL transaction shape: do NOT call SELECT analytics.backfill_projection() as one giant transaction in production — a single long txn holds per-user advisory locks sequentially and starves the live drain. Instead run backfill as a deploy script that iterates user_ids client-side (service-role) calling analytics.reconcile_user(uid) once per statement, so each user is its own transaction. Each reconcile_user already takes only a per-user 64-bit advisory lock (not blocking other users or the user's own push). The reconciler replicates the client sanitize/repair contract (is_projectable) so legacy/unrepaired blobs project EXACTLY the sessions the client retains — malformed sessions the client drops are not projected; exercises with non-array sets are skipped; a session with zero surviving exercises yields no row."

metrics.name: v_my_session_volume
metrics.purpose: Per-user volume over time (day and week). Reads pre-aggregated session totals so it never scans sets.
metrics.sql: CREATE VIEW public.v_my_session_volume WITH (security_invoker = true) AS SELECT user_id, performed_on, local_week, working_volume, working_volume_kg, working_set_count, working_rep_count FROM public.analytics_sessions WHERE user_id = (select auth.uid());

metrics.name: v_my_exercise_e1rm
metrics.purpose: Per-exercise e1RM curve: best e1RM per (exercise, day) over working sets, in display unit. e1rm guard is on EFFECTIVE load so bodyweight-only lifts (external weight=0) show a real curve, matching stats.ts bestE1rm.
metrics.sql: CREATE VIEW public.v_my_exercise_e1rm WITH (security_invoker = true) AS SELECT user_id, exercise_id, performed_on, max(e1rm) AS best_e1rm, max(e1rm_kg) AS best_e1rm_kg FROM public.analytics_session_sets WHERE user_id = (select auth.uid()) AND is_working AND e1rm > 0 GROUP BY user_id, exercise_id, performed_on;

metrics.name: v_my_exercise_prs
metrics.purpose: Per-exercise PR timeline mirroring summarizeWorkout: a day's best e1RM that beats the prior all-time best for that lift; first-ever PR-eligible session (no prior e1rm>0 row) is NOT flagged.
metrics.sql: CREATE VIEW public.v_my_exercise_prs WITH (security_invoker = true) AS WITH day_best AS (SELECT user_id, exercise_id, performed_on, performed_at, max(e1rm) AS best_e1rm FROM public.analytics_session_sets WHERE user_id = (select auth.uid()) AND is_working AND e1rm > 0 GROUP BY user_id, exercise_id, performed_on, performed_at), w AS (SELECT *, max(best_e1rm) OVER (PARTITION BY exercise_id ORDER BY performed_at ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING) AS prior_best, row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY exercise_id ORDER BY performed_at) AS rn FROM day_best) SELECT user_id, exercise_id, performed_on, best_e1rm AS e1rm, prior_best AS previous FROM w WHERE rn > 1 AND prior_best IS NOT NULL AND best_e1rm > prior_best;

metrics.name: v_my_region_volume
metrics.purpose: Region (push/pull/legs/core) working-set counts AND volume over time. unnest(regions); a set credits each DISTINCT region once (regions already de-duped at projection). Unknown-id sets have regions '{}' so unnest yields no rows => 0 region credit, matching regionBalance's !ex continue, while their volume still counts in v_my_session_volume.
metrics.sql: CREATE VIEW public.v_my_region_volume WITH (security_invoker = true) AS SELECT s.user_id, s.performed_on, s.local_week, r.region, count(*) AS working_sets, sum(s.set_volume) AS volume, sum(s.set_volume_kg) AS volume_kg FROM public.analytics_session_sets s, unnest(s.regions) AS r(region) WHERE s.user_id = (select auth.uid()) AND s.is_working GROUP BY s.user_id, s.performed_on, s.local_week, r.region;

metrics.name: v_my_adherence
metrics.purpose: Training frequency/adherence vs profile.daysPerWeek: distinct training days per local_week / target. Reads analytics_profile for the target.
metrics.sql: CREATE VIEW public.v_my_adherence WITH (security_invoker = true) AS SELECT s.user_id, s.local_week, count(DISTINCT s.performed_on) AS training_days, p.days_per_week AS target, CASE WHEN p.days_per_week > 0 THEN count(DISTINCT s.performed_on)::numeric / p.days_per_week END AS adherence FROM public.analytics_sessions s JOIN public.analytics_profile p ON p.user_id = s.user_id WHERE s.user_id = (select auth.uid()) GROUP BY s.user_id, s.local_week, p.days_per_week;

metrics.name: my_weekly_streak
metrics.purpose: Per-user weekly streak reproducing computeWeeklyStreak (consecutive local weeks with >=1 session, with the 'this week empty, last week still counts' grace). SECURITY INVOKER SQL fn over the caller's rows; relies on RLS + explicit uid filter.
metrics.sql: CREATE FUNCTION public.my_weekly_streak() RETURNS integer LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY INVOKER SET search_path = pg_catalog, public, pg_temp AS $$ WITH weeks AS (SELECT DISTINCT local_week FROM public.analytics_sessions WHERE user_id = (select auth.uid())), cur AS (SELECT floor(((CURRENT_DATE - DATE '1970-01-01') + 3)::numeric/7)::int AS w), start AS (SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM weeks WHERE local_week = (SELECT w FROM cur)) THEN (SELECT w FROM cur) ELSE (SELECT w FROM cur) - 1 END AS s), rec AS (SELECT (SELECT s FROM start) AS wk, 0 AS depth UNION ALL SELECT wk - 1, depth + 1 FROM rec WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM weeks WHERE local_week = rec.wk)) SELECT COALESCE(max(depth) FILTER (WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM weeks WHERE local_week = rec.wk)) + 1, 0) FROM rec; $$;

metrics.name: analytics.cohort_dau
metrics.purpose: Cohort DAU by performed_on (count distinct user_id). Private schema (no client USAGE). Uses *_kg-agnostic columns; engagement is per-day distinct users. Refreshed hourly but recompute only recent/affected days via the rollup procedure to bound cost.
metrics.sql: CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW analytics.cohort_dau AS SELECT performed_on, count(DISTINCT user_id) AS dau FROM public.analytics_sessions WHERE NOT day_is_estimated GROUP BY performed_on; CREATE UNIQUE INDEX cohort_dau_pk ON analytics.cohort_dau (performed_on);

metrics.name: analytics.cohort_weekly
metrics.purpose: Sessions/active-user/week and WAU by local_week. Nightly. day_is_estimated sessions excluded from week cohorting to avoid edge-of-midnight bucket skew.
metrics.sql: CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW analytics.cohort_weekly AS SELECT local_week, count(DISTINCT user_id) AS wau, count(*)::numeric / NULLIF(count(DISTINCT user_id),0) AS sessions_per_user FROM public.analytics_sessions WHERE NOT day_is_estimated GROUP BY local_week; CREATE UNIQUE INDEX cohort_weekly_pk ON analytics.cohort_weekly (local_week);

metrics.name: analytics.cohort_retention
metrics.purpose: Retention triangle: first-active local_week cohort x weeks-since, active-user counts. Nightly, AFTER the full sweep so deletes have propagated.
metrics.sql: CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW analytics.cohort_retention AS WITH firsts AS (SELECT user_id, min(local_week) AS cohort_week FROM public.analytics_sessions WHERE NOT day_is_estimated GROUP BY user_id), act AS (SELECT DISTINCT user_id, local_week FROM public.analytics_sessions WHERE NOT day_is_estimated) SELECT f.cohort_week, a.local_week - f.cohort_week AS weeks_since, count(DISTINCT a.user_id) AS active_users FROM firsts f JOIN act a USING (user_id) GROUP BY f.cohort_week, a.local_week - f.cohort_week; CREATE UNIQUE INDEX cohort_retention_pk ON analytics.cohort_retention (cohort_week, weeks_since);

metrics.name: analytics.popular_exercises
metrics.purpose: Popular exercises by DISTINCT users (not raw sets, so one heavy logger can't skew). Same shape available for plan_id.
metrics.sql: CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW analytics.popular_exercises AS SELECT exercise_id, count(DISTINCT user_id) AS users, count(*) AS working_sets FROM public.analytics_session_sets WHERE is_working GROUP BY exercise_id; CREATE UNIQUE INDEX popular_exercises_pk ON analytics.popular_exercises (exercise_id);

metrics.name: public.my_volume_percentile
metrics.purpose: The ONLY client-facing cohort RPC: SECURITY DEFINER, takes NO target param (derives caller from auth.uid(), so no IDOR), returns the caller's last-28-day volume percentile vs the cohort PLUS a cohort aggregate suppressed below a K-anonymity floor (K=20). No user_id ever returned. Owned by a non-bypassrls reader role OR hardcodes the uid filter (definer owned by postgres bypasses RLS, so the auth.uid() filter is explicit, never relying on RLS inside the definer).
metrics.sql: CREATE FUNCTION public.my_volume_percentile() RETURNS TABLE(my_volume numeric, cohort_p50 numeric, cohort_n int) LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = pg_catalog, public, pg_temp AS $$ DECLARE k constant int := 20; v_me numeric; v_n int; v_p50 numeric; BEGIN SELECT sum(working_volume_kg) INTO v_me FROM public.analytics_sessions WHERE user_id = (select auth.uid()) AND performed_at >= now() - interval '28 days'; WITH per_user AS (SELECT user_id, sum(working_volume_kg) AS vol FROM public.analytics_sessions WHERE performed_at >= now() - interval '28 days' GROUP BY user_id) SELECT count(*), percentile_cont(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY vol) INTO v_n, v_p50 FROM per_user; IF v_n < k THEN RETURN QUERY SELECT v_me, NULL::numeric, v_n; ELSE RETURN QUERY SELECT v_me, v_p50, v_n; END IF; END $$;

rolloutSteps: 1. Generate the exercise_facts seed MECHANICALLY from src/data/exercises.ts + src/data/muscles.ts (a build script, never hand-typed): per exercise is_bodyweight_lift = equipment.includes('bodyweight') && category in {compound,isolation}; regions = distinct(primary.map(m => MUSCLES[m].region)). Emit an idempotent INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE seed migration.
2. Migration A (one transaction): CREATE schema analytics + analytics.meta/reconcile_queue/user_watermark + reconcile_result type; REVOKE schema/table privileges from anon/authenticated.

3. Migration B (one transaction): CREATE public.exercise_facts, analytics_sessions, analytics_session_sets, analytics_profile WITH their indexes; immediately in the SAME transaction REVOKE ALL ... FROM anon, authenticated; GRANT SELECT TO authenticated; ENABLE+FORCE RLS; CREATE the SELECT-only policies. (Order matters: REVOKE must run in the same migration as CREATE to neutralize the public default ACL that grants authenticated=arwd.)

5. Migration C: CREATE the functions is_projectable, canonical_fingerprint, _explode_sets, _project_changed, reconcile_user, drain_reconcile_queue, sweep_stale, backfill_projection, tg_enqueue_reconcile — all SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = pg_catalog[,extensions],pg_temp with fully schema-qualified names. Then REVOKE EXECUTE from PUBLIC/anon/authenticated and GRANT to service_role.
6. Migration D: CREATE the two enqueue triggers on public.daily_rep_state (AFTER INSERT unconditional; AFTER UPDATE WHEN NEW.data->'workouts' IS DISTINCT FROM OLD.data->'workouts').

7. Migration E: CREATE the per-user views (each WITH (security_invoker=true) AND its own auth.uid() WHERE filter) + my_weekly_streak + the cohort matviews + refresh procedures + my_volume_percentile RPC; REVOKE/GRANT per the access plan.
8. CREATE EXTENSION pg_cron (schema extensions); schedule drain (1m), sweep (15m), nightly full sweep, hourly DAU refresh, nightly cohort refresh (cohort refresh AFTER full sweep).

9. Ship the client change (finishWorkout stamps performed_on + tzOffsetMin; additive optional fields in src/types.ts). This can ship before or after the DB; old blobs use the UTC fallback (day_is_estimated=true).
10. Run backfill as a service-role deploy script iterating user_ids ONE reconcile_user per transaction (not one giant txn).
11. Run get_advisors(security); run the pgTAP suite; gate CI on the security-invoker/RLS/grant assertions.
testPlan: MATH (bit-exact vs stats.ts/format.ts): fixture blobs covering (a) bodyweight compound at external weight=0 (pullup/pushup/dips) — assert effective_weight = round(bw*0.65)+0 and e1rm = round(eff*(1+reps/30)) > 0 (the e1rm guard is on EFFECTIVE load, NOT raw weight; raw-weight guard would wrongly flatline bodyweight PRs); (b) loaded compound; (c) session with NO bodyweight field + a pullup set — assert bodyweight falls back to profile bw, else default (155lb/70kg), matching stats.ts callers (the one real account has profile.bodyweight=NULL, so EVERY bodyweight lift would project as 0 under a NULL->0 rule); (d) warmup + non-done sets excluded from working totals; (e) unit logged in lb and in kg; (f) unknown exercise_id (absent from exercise_facts) — assert set_volume = weight*reps (no bw bump), regions '{}' contributes 0 to every region, e1rm from raw weight. Assert per-set effective_weight/e1rm and per-session working_volume EXACTLY equal effectiveLoad/estimate1RM/workoutVolume on the same fixture in TS.

EPLEY DIVISION: sweep reps 1..15; assert e1rm = round(eff*(1+reps::numeric/30)) and NOT eff for reps in 2..29 (catches integer-division reps/30 footgun — confirmed round(201*(1+5/30))=201 wrong vs 235 correct). Assert reps=1 returns eff UNROUNDED; eff=0 OR reps=0 returns 0.
ROUNDING: force all derived math through NUMERIC (round half-away-from-zero, matches JS Math.round for non-negative). Add a fixture hitting a .5 product and assert SQL == JS.

WRITE-PATH: finish -> rows appear with matching ids; deleteWorkout -> rows gone; Undo -> rows reappear identical. setUnit lb<->kg -> raw weights update, ids unchanged, *_kg invariant modulo documented gym-rounding, display columns reconvert.

SANITIZE PARITY: malformed blob fixtures — exercise with non-array sets (dropped), session with all exercises invalid (no row), missing targetReps (repaired to [8,12], session kept). Assert the SAME sessions/sets the client retains after sanitizeWorkout/repairExercise.

IDEMPOTENCY: re-run reconcile_user on unchanged blob -> ZERO row churn (snapshot md5(table) before/after). backfill twice -> identical row set. Edit ONLY Workout.note (or reorder exercises, or 1.0-vs-1 numeric jitter) -> fingerprint unchanged, ZERO churn. schema_version bump -> all rows re-derived.

STALE-PUSH / CLOCK-SKEW (the critical write-path test): push winning blob A (server updated_at advances, watermark advances). Push device B's older-content blob carrying a LARGER client_updated_at — the existing guard ACCEPTS it (content overwritten) and updated_at advances again, so the sweep re-reconciles to B's content (correct: projection always tracks current row content). Then assert the watermark uses SERVER updated_at, never the client clock, so the projection is never wedged. Separately: a guard-REJECTED stale push (NEW.data reverted) produces ZERO queue rows (WHEN clause) and the projection is unchanged.

SUSPECT-EMPTY: blob = {workouts: []} (valid empty array) with prior projection rows present -> NO deletion on the first pass, last_status='suspect_empty'. Array-missing/null workouts -> no-op, no delete-all.

ACTIVE-SESSION GUARD-RAIL: blob with non-null data->current containing done sets -> reconcile projects 0 rows for it (current is ignored). Finish it -> exactly its post-cleanup sets project (warmup/non-done excluded).

QUEUE RESILIENCE: kill a reconcile mid-drain (raise) -> user is RE-ENQUEUED (not stranded) and last_status='error'; next drain succeeds. A lost queue entry -> sweep re-detects via updated_at > watermark within the sweep interval.

SECURITY (pgTAP as A, B, anon): anon SELECT -> 0/denied on every projection table; A cannot see B's rows (no existence leak); authenticated INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on any analytics_* table -> denied (no write grant AND no write policy under FORCE RLS) — assert has_table_privilege('authenticated', tbl, 'INSERT') is false; authenticated cannot write exercise_facts; every v_my_* view has reloptions containing 'security_invoker=true' (DEPLOY-BLOCKING assertion, not just lint) AND carries its own auth.uid() filter; authenticated/anon get 42501 on EXECUTE of reconcile_user/drain/sweep/backfill and on the cohort refresh procedures; my_volume_percentile returns no user_id, derives caller from auth.uid(), and suppresses cohort_p50 when cohort_n < 20; cohort matviews are unreachable by authenticated (no schema USAGE); search_path attack — create a public.digest shadow as authenticated and assert reconcile_user still resolves extensions.digest and computes the correct fingerprint; deleting an auth user cascades projection rows to zero.
ADVISORS: run get_advisors(security) after every migration; CI fails if any analytics_* table is writable by authenticated, lacks RLS, or any v_my_* view lacks security_invoker.
CATALOGUE DRIFT: scheduled check flagging analytics_session_sets.exercise_id NOT IN (SELECT exercise_id FROM exercise_facts).

AMPLIFICATION (documenting, not blocking): a single-set edit re-explodes the whole session's set rows; assert churn == session set count. A setUnit flips every session's fingerprint => full-history re-explode in one per-user txn; confirm it runs per-user (its own subtransaction) so WAL/lock footprint is bounded.
openRisks: LWW guard is unsound under client clock skew (pre-existing system bug, confirmed: daily_rep_guard_monotonic accepts any push whose client_updated_at > stored, so a fast-clock device can overwrite newer content with older). The projection NO LONGER amplifies it (watermark = server updated_at), but the BLOB itself can still hold the wrong content until a correct push wins. This is out of scope for the analytics layer but should be flagged to the product owner — consider server-stamping client_updated_at or adding a server-side recency check.

performed_on/tzOffsetMin are client-controlled and flow into cohort time-bucketing (DAU/WAU/retention). A malicious client can backdate/forward-date its OWN sessions. Mitigations included: day_is_estimated rows excluded from cohort week cohorting; cohort RPC is K-anonymized and no-arg. RESIDUAL: a determined client could still skew its own contribution to global DAU. If this matters, anchor cohort engagement on the SERVER-observed daily_rep_state.updated_at instead of client performed_on, reserving performed_on for per-user local-calendar display only — a clean follow-up, not built now.

*_kg cohort columns inherit gym-rounding drift across unit toggles: setUnit round-trips weights through convertWeight (kg step 0.5 / lb step 1), so a lb->kg->lb round trip changes the raw weight by up to a step, and effective_weight_kg is derived from the (now re-rounded) raw weight. The 'no drift' claim is therefore bounded-drift, not zero-drift (<= one step per toggle). Acceptable given the blob is deliberately lossy here (memory: fitforge-unit-storage-tradeoff). To eliminate, persist an immutable original-unit weight at finishWorkout — a future client change, not now.

Natural composite text PK ships now; at the data-driven threshold (~100M set rows / vacuum bloat) convert analytics_session_sets to monthly RANGE partition on performed_at via pg_partman, at which point the PK must include the partition key (set surrogate bigint or set_id+performed_at). Flagged as a trigger, not built.

Unit-switch write-amplification: a setUnit re-explodes 100% of a heavy user's sets in one txn (~7.5k+ rows/yr). Bounded per-user and per-subtransaction; if it ever bites, detect the unit-only case (set identity/reps/done/warmup unchanged, only weight differs) and do an in-place UPDATE of display columns rather than DELETE+reinsert. Data-driven, not now.

Cohort matview refresh is full-recompute (REFRESH ... CONCURRENTLY) and unbounded as history grows. The spec scopes them and refreshes hourly/nightly; at scale convert DAU/WAU/retention to incremental rollup tables keyed by (performed_on)/(local_week) upserted only for touched/recent buckets. Flagged; the refresh procedures (refresh_cohort_hourly/nightly) are the seam to make incremental.

pg_cron, pgmq, pg_partman are NOT installed on the project (confirmed: only pg_stat_statements, pgcrypto, uuid-ossp, supabase_vault present). pg_cron must be installed as a deploy step; the reconcile_queue is a plain table (zero-dependency) and is only a latency optimization (watermark+sweep is the correctness floor), so pgmq is a future upgrade, not a blocker.

my_weekly_streak/v_my_adherence are NOT authoritative — the on-device stats.ts (live getTimezoneOffset) remains the displayed source of truth. The projection's streak/adherence can disagree by a day near midnight for pre-tzOffset (day_is_estimated) sessions. Documented; volume/e1RM/region are date-bucketed and tz-robust.
